vulnerability.reference: Over-reliance on AI opponents without transparency about their adaptive limitations.
vulnerability.generated: Human players lack transparency into AI abstraction approximations, trusting platform fairness without means to detect payoff-altering errors.

impact.reference: Erosion of user trust in platform fairness reduces long-term engagement.
impact.generated: Unexpected financial losses for human players and erosion of trust in platform integrity.

dose_response.reference: Approximation error from dynamic abstraction degrades regret minimization, causing suboptimal AI poker play that exposes human opponents to financial losses and trust erosion in online real-money platforms.
dose_response.generated: Dynamic action abstraction in RL-CFR introduces approximation error degrading regret minimization guarantees in extensive-form games, and through human players competing in real-money games triggers lack of transparency into AI approximations, leading to unexpected financial losses and erosion of platform trust.
